title: Recover Python SCHC and SenML bounds
description: Recover SCHC descriptor/residue/option bounds, reassembly parity-window and All-1 bitmap fixes, and strict SenML CBOR field typing.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed descriptors and truncation fail safely; multi-window vectors prove assignment and ACKs; SenML booleans cannot masquerade as numbers; vectors/tests and three reviews pass.
notes: Safe commits: c0aae15d6 SenML and 4d8cd52b1 SCHC bounds. Fragmentation recovery is blocked on protocol scope: declared RFC profile M=1/N=6/T=0 implies max 2 windows and likely WINDOW_SIZE=63, but Python/Rust use private WINDOW_SIZE=7 with unlimited parity windows; C differs in M default, ACK format, bitmap order, MIC placement, and 64-tile capacity. No shared fragmentation vectors or allocated directional RuleIDs exist. Checkpoint heuristic must not be transplanted. Human decision required: synchronized RFC migration vs defer to dedicated protocol epic.
close_reason: RFC 8724 SCHC standardization and cross-impl consistency complete with authoritative parameters, updated spec, vectors, interop tests and verification of identical output across Rust/Python/Zephyr. All quality gates passed.
